title: Seed treatment
abstract: The present invention relates generally to the field of agriculture. In particular, the invention relates to seed treatment compositions, to a method for preparing the compositions and to their use, and to seeds coated in or treated with the compositions and to plants generated from the seeds. The seed treatment compositions comprise one or more aromatic amino acids and one or more gibberellins.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A seed treatment composition comprising one or more aromatic amino acids and one or more gibberellins. 2. Composition according to claim 1 , wherein said aromatic amino acid is one or more of the following: phenylalanine (Phe or F), tryptophan (Trp or W) and tyrosine (Tyr or Y), which amino acids may be in L- and/or D-form. 3. Composition according to claim 1 or 2, wherein said one or more gibberellin comprises gibberellic acid (GA3). 4. Composition according to any preceding claim, wherein the concentration of the aromatic amino acid is in the range of from about 0.0001 to about 10.0 mg/kg seed, or in the range of from about 0.001 to about 5.0 mg/kg seed, or in the range of from about 0.001 to about lmg/kg seed or in range of from about 0.001 to about O. lmg/kg seed. 5. Composition according to any preceding claim, wherein said gibberellin is preferably GAB and is present in the range of from about 0 to about 200.0 mg/kg seed, or in the range of from about 1.0 to about 120.0 mg/kg seed, or in the range of from about 4.0 to about 80.0 mg/kg seed, or preferably in the range of from about 8.0 to about 40.0 mg/kg seed. 6. Composition according to any preceding claim comprising the combination of tryptophan in the range of from about 0.005 to about O. lmg/kg seed and GA3 at a concentration in the range of from about 8.0 to about 40mg/kg seed. 7. Composition for the treatment or coating of seeds comprising tryptophan at a concentration in the range of from about 0.005 to about O. lmg/kg seed. 8. Composition according to any preceding claim further comprising one or more plant protection, plant health or plant growth products, such as one or more pesticide, herbicide, fertilizer, nutrient, adjuvant, vitamin, growth regulator, and dye. 9. A method for the preparation of a seed treatment composition, comprising mixing one or more aromatic amino acids with one or more gibberellins, in any order. 10. A method for the application of a seed treatment composition as defined in any of claims 1 to 8 or a method for coating or treating a seed with a seed treatment composition as defined in any of claims 1 to 8, comprising coating or directly or indirectly contacting said composition with a seed. 11. A treated or coated seed comprising the seed treatment composition according to any one of claims 1 to 8 or a seed obtainable from the method of claim 10. 12. Seed according to claim 11, wherein said seed is from a monocotyledonous plant or dicotyledonous plant, preferably cereals (for example, spring and winter wheat, maize, triticale, rice, barley, rye, oats, sorghum, millet); grasses (for example, turf grass, rye grass, fescue, dwarf perennial rye grass, strong creeping red fescue); commercially important crop plants (for example, sugar cane, sugar beet, cotton, canola, sunflower, mustard, corn, coffee, tea, tobacco); legumes (for example, soybeans or beans); members of the solanaceae family (for example, tomatoes, peppers and potatoes); greenhouse crops; tree crops (such as pome and stone fruit crops, and nut crops such as walnuts, pistachio and olives, coco pods, palms such as 011 palm and date palm; vegetables such as brassicas for instance cabbages and lettuces. 13. A plant obtainable from a seed according to claim 11 or 12. 14. A method for growing a plant from a treated or coated seed according to claim 11 or 12 or a method for obtaining plants having improved growth characteristics, comprising the steps of: a) sowing said coated seed or growing said treated seed; and 

 b) growing a plant from said seed, optionally under abiotic and/or biotic stress conditions, c) obtaining plants having improved growth characteristics. 15. A kit comprising: a) one or more aromatic amino acids and one or more gibberellins; or b) a composition as defined in any of claims 1 to 8; and optionally c) an agriculturally acceptable carrier; and optionally d) an applicator for applying any of elements a), b) or c) to a seed. 16. Use of one or more aromatic amino acids and one or more gibberellins in improving seed performance and/or in improving the growth characteristics of plants resulting from treated or coated seeds. 17. Use of a composition as defined in any of claims 1 to 8 or use of a kit according to claim 15 in improving seed performance and/or in improving the growth characteristics of plants resulting from coated or treated seeds. 18. Use of a composition according to claim 6 for obtaining a synergistic effect, especially on aboveground plant biomass. 19. Use according to any one of claims 16 to 18, wherein said improved seed performance and/or improved growth characteristics may be manifested by, for example, any one or more of the following: increased percentage germination and/or emergence, increased speed of germination and/or emergence, improved uniformity of germination and/or emergence, increased plant establishment, increased percentage of seedlings that develop and survive, enhanced vigour, early vigour, increased fresh weight of seedlings and/or resultant plants, increased tillering, increased productive tillers, increased biomass (aboveground or belowground), increased yield (of aboveground parts, including seeds, or of belowground plant parts, including tubers, rhizomes, roots etc.), increased abiotic stress tolerance, increased biotic stress tolerance, reduction in crop losses.
